content_body:
Winter in Blue Ridge, Georgia can be a magical time for kids. From family-friendly hikes to gem mining, Blue Ridge offers plenty of things to do to keep your little ones entertained while engaging their imaginative spirit. Here are a few ideas for winter things to do in Blue Ridge for kids ~

Explore the Senses in Downtown Blue Ridge 

Main Street in Downtown Blue Ridge is the perfect playground for kids and families boasting several hole-in-the-wall shops and attractions that will keep everyone engaged for hours. When you begin your day on Main Street, start at 2nd Story Entertainment for some indoor mini golf built inside a 100 year old historic hotel! After building up an appetite from your sporting adventures, work your way down Main Street and stop by The Sweet Shoppe for an award-winning cupcake and a latte for a little pick-me-up. Once you meander your way through the shops, make sure to visit Huck's General Store for some old fashioned and classic candy, toys, kitchen items, and other Blue Ridge souvenirs. Finally, cap off your fun-filled day by visiting Vertigo Pinball for an adult beverage and some snacks while your kids expend their energy playing some pinball! From one end of Main Street to the other, Downtown Blue Ridge, GA boasts so many family-friendly activities to bring out your nostalgia as an adult while offering new experiences and memories for the younger ones. 

Make Their Cowboy and Cowgirl Dreams Come True and Go Horseback Riding

Connect your child's imagination with reality and let them live out their visions of being a cowboy or cowgirl exploring the frontier with Appalachian Trail Rides or Blue Ridge Mountain Trail Rides! Exploring the great outdoors on horseback is a thrilling experience, especially with the mountain air and gorgeous views. Reserving a guided trail ride through the beautiful natural surroundings allows you to reconnect with the sights and sounds of nature in the North Georgia Mountains.

Chase a Magical Waterfall 

Experience the beauty of Fall Branch Falls, located in the heart of the Aska Adventure Area. This short hike winds through the woods to reveal a cascading waterfall with some of the clearest water you will ever see. Open your child's eyes to the wonder of nature on this excursion!

Pretend They're Walking the Plank At the Swinging Bridge

A family favorite for many years, The Swinging Bridge is a local treasure tucked away in the national forest and on the Benton MacKaye Trail and is a perfect North Georgia hike for all ages. Unleash your child's inner pirate or Peter Pan and pretend they are walking the plank over this 265' long suspension bridge. Once you reach the other side, explore a few small waterfalls, the riverbank, and the stunning forest around you! 

Mine for Precious Stones 

Also in the Aska Adventure Area, gem mining is the perfect attraction to entertain your kids! Both Lily Pad Village and Aska Mining Company offer incredible gem mining experiences that will leave your kids reminiscing for months. In case you needed more of an incentive, they get to keep the gems they uncover! 

Spend Quality Time Together and Break Out Your Favorite Board Games

After a long, exciting day in Blue Ridge, return to your welcoming, luxury cabin for quality time with the family. Enjoy some friendly competition with your favorite board games or game tables in your cabin, roast s'mores in the outdoor fireplace, or turn on your favorite movie. 

With these activities and more, Blue Ridge is the perfect destination for winter fun with the whole family. Bundle up and head to the mountains for a memorable winter vacation!
title: Best Winter Activities For Kids in Blue Ridge
